authority.guide: Hospitality & Entertainment Guide (LCC Chart of Accounts PDF, pp 68-70)

fbt_method.name: 50/50 method for meal entertainment
fbt_method.rule: Council uses the 50/50 method to determine FBT liability for meal entertainment. All meal entertainment (whether for employees or non-employees) is subject to FBT at 50% of the value of the cost. Using the 50/50 method removes the need to analyse actual expenditure.

reason_codes.A: Food is provided for a work-related purpose rather than an entertainment purpose.
reason_codes.B: Food is provided for the purpose of refreshment rather than an entertainment purpose.
reason_codes.C: Social function (recreation or meal entertainment). These are subject to FBT.
reason_codes.D: Expense benefits with a value of < $300 (including GST). These are minor benefits if provided infrequently and are exempt.
reason_codes.E: Expense benefits with a value of $300 or more. These are subject to FBT.
reason_codes.F: Light refreshments provided with work-related training.
reason_codes.G: Non work-related meal entertainment (e.g. for guest speaker, volunteer) or gift.
reason_codes.H: Requires assessment of a combination of factors (purpose / type of food / when / where).
reason_codes.I: Years of Service Gift < $500. FBT exempt.
reason_codes.J: Years of Service Gift > $500. Subject to FBT.
reason_codes.K: Event or gift for community members/non-employees. Exempt from FBT.
reason_codes.L: Presenter, speaker, entertainer's fee — non-FBT if for a community event. Exempt from FBT (account 73515).

internal_catering.context: Catering provided by Council owned and operated facilities (Logan Entertainment Centre and Logan Metro InSports)
internal_catering.invoice_requirements: Full details of catering provided, including purpose for catering, internal/external attendees and specifics of food and drink provided must be included on any internal invoice quoting natural accounts 7B221/7B222.
internal_catering.authorisation: Authorised per Instrument of Authorisation AUTH0009 (Financial Authorisation Limits) and AUTH0012 (General Authorisations).

gst_codes.C: Standard taxable supply with credit (most allocations).
gst_codes.P: Generally used for 73511 allocations. Applied where input tax credit not claimable. Final code depends on supplier GST registration, GST status of the supply, and FBT applicability.
gst_codes.note: GST is highly complex; the Finance Branch has the authoritative call. Generally use P for 73511 and C elsewhere.

scenario_to_fbt_data_id_map._comment: Maps fbt_classification.json scenario id to fbt_data.json key (the existing 24-entry operational lookup)
